subject: Geography
title: Stateless Peoples
description: A micro-lesson that makes Stateless Peoples usable.

hook.text: Over 10 million people worldwide are stateless—they belong to no country at all.

buildup.text: The Rohingya, Bidoon, and many Roma communities have no recognized nationality.

discovery.text: Without citizenship, they cannot vote, work legally, own property, or travel.

twist.text: Some people are stateless because borders shifted around them—they didn't move, the lines did.

climax.text: Borders define who belongs. If no border claims you, you become invisible.

punchline.text: Ten million people exist between the lines, belonging nowhere.

quiz.question: How many people worldwide are estimated to be stateless?
